channel.title: Comments for page "...Like Clockwork Hub"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "...Like Clockwork Hub"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Although I had joined the site around April 2017, I didn't start participating in the community and becoming fully engaged until around November of that year. This tale series was being published at the time, and I distinctly remember eagerly waiting for new installments to be published. They were some of the best articles that I had read at the time, and even now, three years later, there has been little else published to change that. This is one of my absolute favorite tale series on the site, one that I have reread many times. Thank you for writing it.
Gosh, I'm glad people liked it this much. Thank you everyone. It means a lot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Was thinking about this series again today- the fact that it's so underrated is just a travesty. If there's one piece I can pretty unambiguously say is "too good" for this place, it's this series.

You did a phenomenal job, Deci, and should be very proud.

item.title: Translations (+ Minor Update)
item.content:encoded:
To promote translations and readings of my work and thank all the authors for their hard work, I am linking to them here in addition to my author page.

Translations

  • Chinese by MottHery
  • Japanese by szh_tmh

Thank you all!

I added two related tales to this series to the hub. I don't think this tale series is strictly a "canon", and these are tales from the CN branch, but they cover enough similar themes and also are good in their own right, so I wanted to give these tales eyes. MottHery also has been translating this series into Chinese :3

I might go through all these tales and do little cleanups here and there all over. That'll be a more gradual process. Also that 3999-J tale will just be an extremely-late last-year Christmas present and an on-time this-year Christmas present.

item.title: 1/17/18 Update
item.content:encoded: Still working on that 3999-J tale! In the meantime, I made several minor edits for the series that, while not wholly significant (save for distinguishing between some characters in a couple of tales), should improve the overall quality of life of these articles.

item.title: Author's Overview
item.content:encoded:
So yeah, when I started this series, I was in a dire spot and just had to write something. I didn't care what its rating was, so long as it was above 0. And now, I have my first finished tale series on the site, one of the most ambitious and unusual ones, and about an SCP I had formerly considered impossible to write tales about until now. So I'm proud of myself.

This series is largely not gonna be a work in progress, but the final tale may be. It took me an incredibly long time to write, edit, and pace, so errors may appear in there that weren't in the others.

I'll post a tale about 3999-J before the end of the month, because by this point I feel like I might as well go the whole distance.

Thanks to the following:

LordStonefish for being, for my money, the most influential writer on this site and writing an SCP deserving of all the praise and analysis it gets. Also, for believing in me and my series.
Taffeta for technical help. Without them, this series literally would not have gotten past the first tale.
Randomini and Rejekyll for support technical help.
thefriendlyvandal and v1als for writing Portraits of Your Father and the character Draven Kondraki, respectively (though Vandal did provide some of Draven's characteristics and most/all of mannerisms which I based my Draven off of; I didn't find other written work with Draven posted before Portraits, after all).
Shaggydredlocks for saying the nicest thing that was ever said about my writing in #site19 one night that I'm too embarrassed to repeat here.

+ FAQ
- FAQ

Q: I don't get it!

A: I'm sure there'll be a declassified about it.

Q: Why did you write about yourself?

A: Did you read the sixth tale?

Q: Yes, but I still didn't understand it?

A: My writing is a reflection of myself, my emotions, and my experiences, so I figured writing about me and having Talloran meet me (as was the case when Talloran came into my dreams during a couple nights over the course of the last month) would only be suitable for a series where I broke almost every rule.

Q: What does the Further Reading/Reference Materials mean?

A: Read those articles to figure out! Some connections are blatantly obvious; some aren't. All were used and read extensively in order to incorporate elements, themes, motifs, and sense of scale for the writing, as I needed some form of grounding.

Q: What do the album/song titles in the tale titles and URLs mean?

A: They mean something, but you'll have to figure them out. I will say some are very simple and surface-level (Dying Out West; Site-118 I figured was on the West Coast, or Western United States), some are pretty obtuse (Only Once Away My Son). Good luck!

Q: Is Talloran trans?

A: Non-binary, so; yes. This of course only applies to my tale series for now, but Stonefish was on-board with it and I basically read Talloran as having a hard time grasping the current gender binary for many reasons (read into my tale series to discern some of those!).

Q: Can I write about Hikari Yamada? What's she like?

A: Yeah! She's a doctor who works at Site-118 (used to work at Site-17 with Talloran). She's very cheerful and energetic, and works obscene amounts of hours in order to take care of people and innovate and improve the Foundation's current medical facilities and technology. She hates being called a nurse. She is in her 40s in this series, which places her as being older than Talloran by a minimum of, let's say ten years?

Q: Is this a canon?

A: No. But you can reference characters/traits/mannerisms/story plots etc in your other works. But this isn't a canon; it's a closed tale series.

Q: [other question]

A: Feel free to ask!

- FAQ

item.title: 12/14/17 Update
item.content:encoded: Will need to delay the finale to Friday, maybe Saturday at latest. I have to get it on-point, after all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These are not the droids you're looking for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This series is remarkably original, thought-provoking, and fluent. You did a really good job on this! +1

+ show block
– hide block

Researcher Talloran and The Being

EDIT: Fixed the image.

item.title: 12/7/17 Update
item.content:encoded:
No tale today. As it turns out, when Hearthstone releases a new expansion, I ignore everything else to play it.

I'll shoot for tomorrow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just woke up so I don't have the vocabulary to respond to this quite yet, but take my love for this comment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
These are deep waters. Swimming on the sunlit surfaces is different from diving to the places where the black moon howls.

My son's therapist once told me that I thought about thinking more than any other patient's parent she'd ever known. Oh, doctor, how little you knew. I am a child on the beach, playing with the shiny pebbles. Out beyond the breakers there are leviathans in the abyss.

item.title: Author's Post
item.content:encoded:
A tale series about SCP-3999. That's as good as I can describe it.

Updates Mondays and Thursdays. That's more manageable for me.
